methodsAttempted.query: site:uwaterloo.ca generative AI artificial intelligence policy students ChatGPT academic integrity University of Waterloo
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search located official Waterloo Academic Integrity, graduate guidance, Writing and Communication Centre, Centre for Teaching Excellence, student blog, and copyright pages.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uwaterloo.ca generative AI teaching learning assessment ChatGPT University of Waterloo
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search located the AVPA artificial-intelligence hub, course-outline suggestions, CTE teaching resources, graduate guidance, and a lab-specific blog.

methodsAttempted.query: site:uwaterloo.ca AI tools Copilot ChatGPT privacy security University of Waterloo generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search located the Information Systems & Technology responsible-use page, AI tools page, and AVPA guidance. Firecrawl scraping then failed with insufficient credits, so verified official pages were fetched by direct HTTP.
Direct link extraction from the IST responsible-use page identified the official AI tools, AI system deployment, approval and support, and safeguards pages.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found university-wide Waterloo guidance from Academic Integrity, Current Graduate Students, the Associate Vice-President Academic, and Information Systems & Technology. Student-blog and lab-blog pages were documented but rejected for low policy specificity or course/unit scope.

targets.sourceTitle: Generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) guidance for graduate students and supervisors
UW Course Outline Suggestions for Generative Artificial Intelligence
Responsible Use of AI Tools with University Data

stopConditions: Stop after official Waterloo pages support direct claim candidates.
Do not infer university-wide rules from student blog posts, lab pages, or faculty/course-specific examples.
Preserve source-language evidence snippets and keep source rights caveats.

artifacts.claimText: Waterloo's Academic Integrity page says instructors should state whether AI tools such as ChatGPT are allowed for assignments, tests, or exams, and that students who do not follow those instructor rules are subject to Policy 71 academic-misconduct processes.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Instructor-defined AI-use rules are tied to Policy 71 compliance.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Waterloo's graduate-student GenAI guidance says graduate students should not submit GenAI-produced work for course assignments without explicit course-instructor permission or instruction.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Graduate coursework GenAI use depends on instructor permission.

artifacts.claimText: For units or individuals deploying AI-enabled systems that process University data, Waterloo IST says projects must undergo an Information Risk Assessment and receive Information Steward approval before using University data.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: At present, it's important for instructors to be explicit about whether artificial intelligence or tools like ChatGPT are allowed to be used to complete assignments, tests or exams, and if so, the extent to which it is allowed, and if it should be cited and how to cite it. A student who does not comply with the instructor's rules about the use of such tools will be subject to Policy 71 and an investigation into academic misconduct.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Academic Integrity tells instructors to state whether AI tools are allowed and links noncompliance to Policy 71.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Instructor resources for Artificial Intelligence ChatGPT tools

artifacts.sourceTitle: Generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) guidance for graduate students and supervisors
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: For research, graduate students should consult with their supervisor(s) and advisory committee (if one exists) about the limit and scope of the use of GenAI tools, which is often discipline-dependent. Permitted uses and expectations for using GenAI tools should be communicated between the students and their supervisor(s).
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: Graduate guidance says research GenAI limits and scope should be discussed with supervisors and advisory committees where applicable.

artifacts.sourceTitle: UW Course Outline Suggestions for Generative Artificial Intelligence
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: For instructors who permit generative AI use: Generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) trained using large language models (LLM) or other methods to produce text, images, music, or code, like Chat GPT, DALL-E, or GitHub CoPilot, may be used for assignments in this class with proper documentation, citation, and acknowledgement.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: AVPA sample language permits GenAI use in assignments when documentation, citation, and acknowledgement are required.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Responsible Use of AI Tools with University Data
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: These review processes ensure that any AI tool used with University data is assessed for security, privacy, and contractual risks, whether the tool is standalone, embedded, or part of an AI-enabled system.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: IST says AI tools used with University data are assessed for security, privacy, and contractual risks.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: All AI-enabled system projects must undergo an Information Risk Assessment (IRA). A Privacy Impact Assessment (PIA) may also be required, depending on the nature of the data being collected or processed. Approval from the appropriate Information Steward is required before using any University data, even if that data is publicly available.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: IST says AI-enabled system projects require an IRA, may require a PIA, and need steward approval before University data use.

artifacts.title: University of Waterloo staged AI policy findings
artifacts.summary: Staged findings for University of Waterloo are based on official Academic Integrity, Current Graduate Students, AVPA, and IST pages. The claims cover instructor-defined AI-use rules, graduate GenAI guidance, sample course-outline language, and IST controls for AI tools or AI-enabled systems using University data.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Student-blog and lab-blog pages were rejected as evidence for university-wide policy claims.
Firecrawl page scraping was unavailable due insufficient credits, so official pages were verified by direct HTTP fetch.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
